title: Refactor AppState client map to Vec per pub_id
description:
Rewrite relay/src/state.rs to support multiple simultaneous connections per pub_id.

Change DashMap<String, (ClientTx, u64)> to DashMap<String, Vec<(ClientTx, u64)>> and rewrite all methods.

Algorithm:
1. Change Inner.clients field type to DashMap<String, Vec<(ClientTx, u64)>>
2. connect(): assign seq via fetch_add; push onto vec (or insert new vec); return seq as u64. New signature: pub fn connect(&self, pub_id: &PubId, tx: ClientTx) -> u64
3. disconnect(): new signature pub fn disconnect(&self, pub_id: &PubId, seq: u64). Remove only the entry with matching seq from vec. If vec empty, remove key and also remove rate_limits entry.
4. connection_seq(): return min seq across all vec entries for pub_id; u64::MAX if absent
5. deliver_live(): send to ALL channels for pub_id; return true if any succeeded
6. broadcast(): flatten all vecs to collect all ClientTx, filter exclude by pub_id key
7. broadcast_to_group(): same flatten; delivered set tracks by pub_id not per-channel

Defensive rules:
- Never hold DashMap lock across await
- disconnect: log warn if seq not found, do not panic
- No unwrap() without comment explaining why it cannot fail

Tests in state.rs cfg(test):
- two_connections_same_pub_id_both_receive: connect twice, deliver_live sends to both
- disconnect_one_leaves_other: disconnect by seq, remaining channel still receives
- connection_seq_returns_min: connect twice, connection_seq returns lower seq
- last_disconnect_removes_key: both disconnected, contains_key false
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-relay passes; four new unit tests pass; cargo clippy -p nie-relay clean
